name: model-scope
description: Turn ANY parameterised model into an interactive explorer: move a slider per parameter and watch the simulation update, shown in views the MODEL defines — no fixed graphic or axis. A Bayesian observer draws prior/likelihood/posterior + a central-tendency curve; a neuron draws a trace + raster + f–I curve; a decision model animates a trajectory + RT histogram; a POMDP/saccade/RL/population model draws whatever fits. Two animation idioms: a continuous playhead AND a process-mode STAGE stepper that walks a model's internal pipeline (stimulus → encoding → likelihood → posterior → estimate) step by step. Build a whole PAPER as a few screens (mechanism → condition comparisons → prediction), separating experimental conditions from model parameters and making the claim↔mechanism mapping visible; heavy screens stream via runChunks with a loading overlay. Schema-driven sliders + a simulate()→views contract + a small plotting helper, no build step. Includes a modelbook of canonical families (Bayesian/efficient-coding observers, causal inference, working memory, decision circuits, neurons, RL, psychophysics) grounded in real open source, a reusable code library, and a GUI QC pipeline (static gate + visual checklist + two-axis review).
